methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The sum of all individual user balances for a specific Curves token must exactly equal the tracked total supply for that token.
invariants.attack_scenario: If tokens can be minted, burned, or transferred such that the sum of balances diverges from the total supply, the bonding curve pricing formula will be manipulated, leading to incorrect prices and potential value extraction.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_totalSupply_matches_balances(address subject) public view { uint256 sum = 0; /* iterate users */ assert(sum == curvesTokenSupply[subject]); }

invariants.description: The total supply of an external ERC20 token must exactly equal the number of internal Curves tokens locked in the Curves contract, scaled by 1e18.
invariants.attack_scenario: If decimal math is flawed or tokens can be minted without locking internal tokens, the 1:1 peg is broken, allowing attackers to inflate the ERC20 supply and drain the bonding curve.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_erc20_peg(address subject) public view { address ext = externalCurvesTokens[subject].token; if(ext != address(0)) { assert(CurvesERC20(ext).totalSupply() == curvesTokenBalance[subject][address(this)] * 1e18); } }

invariants.description: The FeeSplitter contract's ETH balance must always be greater than or equal to the sum of all users' claimable fees.
invariants.attack_scenario: If users can acquire tokens without updating their fee offset (e.g., via transfer), they can claim fees from before they owned the tokens, draining the contract and leaving it insolvent for legitimate claimants.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_fee_splitter_solvency() public view { uint256 totalClaimable = 0; /* iterate users and tokens */ assert(address(this).balance >= totalClaimable); }

invariants.description: A user's accumulated claimable fees must not decrease when they perform actions like buying or selling tokens (excluding explicit fee claims).
invariants.attack_scenario: The onBalanceChange function overwrites userFeeOffset without crediting pending fees to unclaimedFees. When a user buys or sells, their previously earned but unclaimed fees are permanently erased.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_claimable_fees_non_decreasing(address token, address user) public { uint256 pre = getClaimableFees(token, user); /* action */ uint256 post = getClaimableFees(token, user); assert(post >= pre); }

invariants.description: When tokens are transferred, the recipient's fee offset must be updated to the current cumulative fee per token to prevent them from claiming past fees.
invariants.attack_scenario: The _transfer function does not call onBalanceChange. A user can transfer tokens to a new address, which will have a userFeeOffset of 0, allowing them to instantly claim all historical fees for those tokens, draining the FeeSplitter.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_fee_offset_updated_on_transfer(address token, address to) public view { assert(tokensData[token].userFeeOffset[to] == tokensData[token].cumulativeFeePerToken); }

invariants.description: The sum of all fee percentages (protocol, subject, referral, holders) must never exceed the maxFeePercent.
invariants.attack_scenario: If access controls fail (e.g., due to the Security.sol modifier bug), an attacker could set fee percentages to >100%, causing the totalFee to exceed the price, leading to underflow or draining of user funds during buys.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_max_fee() public view { assert(feesEconomics.protocolFeePercent + feesEconomics.subjectFeePercent + feesEconomics.referralFeePercent + feesEconomics.holdersFeePercent <= feesEconomics.maxFeePercent); }
